observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require:
6. The General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment which shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), in accordance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
7. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31, of each year, pursuant to Section 119,120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) by Local Government Unit Volume I.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Accounting and Supply Management Office to coordinate in the implementation of the Perpetual Inventory method of recording Inventories. In addition to this, it should be ascertained that necessary ledger cards are kept by the Accounting Office for each inventory, and submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) by the Supply Management Office must be religiously complied with so the former may make necessary actions or adjustments, if any.

In line with this, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to:
9. Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection and post-repair inspection to the properties subjected to repairs and furnish the reports to the COA Auditor within five (5) days from the date of such evaluation/inspection.
10. See to it that a Waste Management Report is prepared when it involves replacement of worn-out spare parts.
11. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain records of the history of repairs of each LGU property.
12. Direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims pertaining to repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles and heavy equipment with lacking supporting documents.

1.6 We recommended that the LCE:
a) Create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its PPE
which shall be made annually and reported on the RPCPPE, in accordance
with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and
b) Require the General Service Office and the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile and up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and
correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor
concerned not later than January 31, of each year, pursuant to Sections
119,120 and 124 of the MNGAS by LGU Volume I.

2.5 In relation to this, we recommended the LCE:
a) Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the physical
count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon.
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road Network
account based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said
account on the Notes to Financial Statements.
c) Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road inventory
and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every
accounting period as well as keep a complete Local Road Network
Property Card for all roads and its components.
d) Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the General
Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects.

3.6 Thus, we reiterated our recommendations that Management:
a) Create an inventory committee to conduct physical inventory of all PPEs
of the Municipality, be it acquired through purchase or donation,
including those constructed by administration found at station, pursuant
to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
b) Instruct the Supply and Property Unit and Accounting Office to
strengthen its system of maintaining its accounting and property records
such as PCs, PPELCs, and RPCPPE to facilitate periodic reconciliation of
these records to ensure reliability of the PPE account balances as
presented in the Financial Statements.
d) Undertake all other procedures prescribed under COA Circular 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020, to come up with the reconciled and correct
balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements; and
e) After inventory taking, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the
updated ledger cards for each item of PPE pursuant to the Section 13 of
the MNGAS by LGU Volume I.

4.6 We recommended that the LCE direct the municipal accountant to faithfully
enforce the submission of report and supporting documents to the auditor
within first ten (10) days of ensuing month in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of
the RRSA and MNGAS for LGUs V. I.

5.5 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that LCE instruct the Accounting
and Supply Management Office to coordinate in the implementation of the
Perpetual Inventory method of recording Inventories. In addition to this, it
should be ascertained that necessary ledger cards are kept by the Accounting
Office for each inventory, and submission of the Report on the Physical Count
of Inventory (RPCI) by the Supply Management Office must be religiously
complied with so the former may make necessary actions or adjustments, if any.

6.5 In line with this, we reiterated our recommendation that the LCE:
a) Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection
and post-repair inspection to the properties subjected to repairs and
furnish the reports to the COA Auditor within five (5) days from the date
of such evaluation/inspection.
b) See to it that a Waste Management Report is prepared when it involves
replacement of worn-out spare parts.
c) Direct the General Services Officer to maintain records of the history of
repairs of each LGU property.

We recommend Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal accountant to strictly enforce the submission of report and supporting documents to the auditor within first ten (10) days of ensuing month in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account RRSA and LGU NGAS Vol. I.

Taking this to consideration, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to compute and record the depreciation of Property Plant Equipment. Compute the depreciation using the straight line method and depreciate it using the table of estimated useful life provided under COA Circular 2017-004.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend the strict compliance with the provisions of Sections 12 and 13 of Republic Act No. 9337, Section 2.57.2 (I) of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018 and Section 4.114 of Revenue Regulations No. 02-98 dated April 17, 1998.
That being said, we recommend the management to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 53 of R.A. No. 9184 in relation to the procedures of Negotiated Procurement.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct and supervise the persons responsible to promptly submit the lacking documents which led to the suspension of the following transactions mentioned above in compliance with Section 7.1.1 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006, dated September 15, 2009. Otherwise, Notice of Disallowance may be issued as prescribed by Section 9.4 of the same circular.

We recommend Local Chief Executive to strictly comply with the limitations for appropriation for discretionary funds provided in Sec. 325 (h) of RA 7160 and refer to the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Use in Local Government Units as prescribed by COA Circular 2002-003 as guide for future purposes. Furthermore, direct the municipal accountant to adjust the accounts inappropriately charged and refer to the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Use in Local Government Units as prescribed by COA Circular 2002-003 as guide for future purposes.

Hence, we recommend that the Honorable Mayor to strictly oversee the preparation and submission of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer based on the LDRRM Plan and Budget approved by the LDRRM Council. Furthermore, direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Local MDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on the Utilization of DRRMF on a regular monthly basis which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with the above quoted rules.

5. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive requires: (a) The General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its properties, plant and equipment which shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); and (b) The reconciliation of the records of the General Service Office and the Municipal Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31, of each year.
observations.managementAction: No Action taken by Management to create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count despite repetitive recommendat ion thus reiterated in this report
observations.reason: Management still failed to create an inventory committee

observations.recommendation: 9. Taking this to consideration, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to compute and record the depreciation of Property Plant Equipment. Compute the depreciation using the straight line method and depreciate it using the table of estimated useful life provided under COA Circular 2017-004.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant committed to prepare any adjustment to record the unrecorded depreciation.
observations.reason: Useful life of some assets cannot be ascertained

observations.recommendation: 10. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the physical count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon (see Annex C); 11. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road Network account based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said account on the Notes to Financial Statements (see Annex B); 12. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road inventory and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every accounting period (See Annex A) as well as keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components (see Annex E). 13. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: No Action Implemented
observations.reason: Management still failed to create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its Road Network and to update records

observations.recommendation:
1.5 In relation to this, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to:
a. Fast track the creation of an inventory committee of the
municipality to conduct the physical count of its Road Network
and prepare a report thereon (see Annex C);
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road
Network account based on the result of the physical count and
disclose the said account on the Notes to Financial Statements
(see Annex B);
c. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road
inventory and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the
end of every accounting period (See Annex A) as well as keep a
complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and
its components (see Annex E).
d. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and
the General Service Officer with the complete description and
cost segregation of read components for road projects.

2.1 We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal
accountant to strictly enforce the submission of report and supporting
documents to the auditor within the first ten (10) days of ensuing month,
in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on
Settlement of Account (RRSA) and Sections 43 and 44 (k) of the Manual
on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local
Government Units, Volume I.

5.0 In view of the foregoing, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to
instruct the Accounting and Supply Management Office to coordinate
in the implementation of the Perpetual Inventory method of recording
Inventories. In addition to this, it should be ascertained that necessary
ledger cards are kept by the Accounting Office for each inventory, and
submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) by
the Supply Management Office must be religiously complied with so the
former may make necessary actions or adjustments, if any.

5.7 In line with this, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to:
a) Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair
inspection and post-repair inspection to the properties subjected to
repairs and furnish the reports to the COA Auditor within five (5)
days from the date of such evaluation/inspection;
b) See to it that a Waste Management Report is prepared when it
involves replacement of worn-out spare parts;
c) Direct the General Services Officer to maintain records of the
history of repairs of each LGU property; and
d) Direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims pertaining
to repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles and heavy equipment
with lacking supporting documents.

6.5 Taking this to consideration, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to
direct the Municipal Accountant to compute and record the
depreciation of Property Plant Equipment. Compute the depreciation
using the straight line method and depreciate it using the table of
estimated useful life provided under COA Circular 2017-004.

That being said, we recommend the management to strictly comply
with the provisions of Section 53 of R.A. No. 9184 in relation to the
procedures of Negotiated Procurement.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend the strict compliance with
the provisions of Sections 12 and 13 of Republic Act No. 9337, Section 2.57.2
(I) of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018 and Section
4.114 of Revenue Regulations No. 02-98 dated April 17, 1998.

Thus, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the
Municipal Accountant/Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of the
above stated circulars to maintain a sound internal control in the
disbursement of government funds.

In relation to this, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to:
a. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the
physical count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon (see
Annex C);
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road Network
account based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said
account on the Notes to Financial Statements (see Annex B);
c. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road inventory
and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every
accounting period (See Annex A) as well as keep a complete Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components (see Annex E);
and
d. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the
General Services Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require:
a. The General Services Officer (GSO) to create an inventory committee to
conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment which shall
be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
b. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal
Accountant to update the property ledger card to reflect the true and
correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor
concerned not later than January 31, of each year.

We recommend Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal accountant
to strictly enforce the submission of report and supporting documents to the auditor
within first ten (10) days of ensuing month, in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the
Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account and Sections 43 and 44 of the
Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local
Government Units, Volume I.

Taking this to consideration, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to
direct the Municipal Accountant to compute and record the depreciation of Property Plant
Equipment. Compute the depreciation using the straight line method and depreciate
it using the table of estimated useful life provided under Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 2017-004 dated December 13, 2017.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct
and supervise the persons responsible to promptly submit the lacking documents
which led to the suspension of the above-cited transactions, in compliance with
Section 7.1.1 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated
September 15, 2009. Otherwise, Notice of Disallowance may be issued as prescribed
by Section 9.4 of the same circular.

observations.managementAction: AAR Adjustment 2018 already reflected in the Financial Statement

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management direct the Accountant to:
a. transfer funds to Other LGU for any financial assistant in compliance with COA Circular 2015-009.
b. direct the accountant to set-up the Receivable account from LGU which are subject for liquidation or outright record as Subsidy to Other Local Government Unit.
c. prepare adjusting entries to correct overstatement of account Subsidy to Other LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR Adjustment 2018 already reflected in the Financial Statement

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of Agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992. Such BRS shall be regularly submitted to the auditor for review and verification.
observations.managementAction: 2018 already submitted to the auditor's office

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the LCE direct:
a. The local budget council to meticulously plan the procurement of the PPA’s funded out of 20% Development Fund and to consider the important development projects in their budget to insure its implementation in accordance with the provisions of JMC 2017-1.
b. The personnel concerned to ensure that the 20% component of IRA Utilization shall be submitted to the Auditor for validation/inspection of projects.
observations.managementAction: AAR Report was 2018 duly submitted to the auditor’s office

observations.recommendation: We recommend Local Chief Executive to strictly comply with the limitations for the appropriation for discretionary funds provided by law. Furthermore, direct the municipal accountant to adjust the accounts inappropriately charged and refer to the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Use in Local Government Units as prescribed by COA Circular 2002-003 as guide for future purposes.
observations.managementAction: AAR Proper
observations.reason: Charges still exceeded the limitation provided by law

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management direct the Accountant to: a. Effect the necessary adjustments/corrections to correct the misstatements in the accounts of the Payable account and henceforth, ensure that JEVs are properly prepared and reviewed prior to recording. b. Adopt the effective monitoring scheme on the submission of liquidation reports and immediate liquidation of outstanding fund transfers.
observations.managementAction: No adjustment entry was prepared and submitted by the accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Accountant to: a. transfer funds to Other LGU for any financial assistant in compliance with COA Circular 2015-009. b. direct the accountant to set-up the Receivable account from the respective LGU which are subject for liquidation. c. prepare adjusting entries to correct overstatement of account Subsidy to Other LGU.
observations.managementAction: No adjustment entries were prepared and submitted by the accountant.
observations.reason: No Receivable account was set up as well as any adjustment entries were prepared and submitted by the accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive requires:

a. the General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to
conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment, and shall be
made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).

b. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal
Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and
correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor
concerned not later than January 31, of each year.

We recommend Head of Agency to instruct

a. the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a
monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4,
1992.

b. update the reconciliation of bank and book balances so that reconciling
items can be accurately

Such BRS shall be regularly submitted to the auditor for review and
verification.

We recommended that management direct the Accountant to:

a. ) effect the necessary adjustments/corrections to correct the misstatements in
the Payable account and henceforth, ensure that JEVs are properly prepared
and reviewed prior to recording. b.) adopt the effective monitoring scheme
on the submission of liquidation reports and immediate liquidation of
outstanding fund transfers.

We recommend that the LCE direct:

a. The local budget council to meticulously plan the procurement of the
PPA’s funded out of 20% Development Fund and to consider the
important development projects in their budget proposal to insure its
implementation in accordance with the provisions of JMC 2017-1.

b. The personnel concerned to ensure that the 20% component of IRA
Utilization shall be submitted to the Auditor for validation/inspection
of projects.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel
concerned to settle their Notice of Suspension, pursuant to Section 9.4 of the
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 September 15, 2009. And
strictly follow the rules and regulations on the disbursement of funds with
complete documentation to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommend that the LCE direct the LDRRFO and Municipal
Accountant to submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Fund Project (LDRRMFP)
and the monthly report of Source and Utilization of DRRMF and strictly observe the
disclosure requirements of Unexpended balance of Quick Response Fund (QRF) and
the MOOE of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statement.
We recommend that Head of Agency to strictly enforce the compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account (RRSA) and Section 179 of GAAM.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive requires:
the General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment, and shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31, of each year.

We recommend that the Head of Agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992.  Such BRS shall be regularly submitted to the auditor for review and verification.
We recommend that Head of Agency to strictly enforce the compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account RRSA and Section 179 of GAAM

We recommend that the Head of Agency shall direct the Accountant and Accountable Officer to submit Disbursement Vouchers for cash advance and strictly comply the provision of COA Circular No. 97-002 particularly Section 5 for liquidation of cash advance and submission of Liquidation Report with all supporting documents to the auditor for audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents. Stop the practice of paying transactions without complete supporting documents.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension and Disallowances if warranted.

We recommend that the LCE direct:
The local budget council to meticulously plan the procurement of the PPA’s funded out of 20% Development Fund and to consider the important development projects in their budget proposal to insure its implementation in accordance with the provisions of JMC 2017-1.
The personnel concerned to ensure that the 20% component of IRA Utilization shall be submitted to the Auditor for validation/inspection of projects.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel concerned to settle their Notice of Suspension, pursuant to Section 9.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 September 15, 2009.  And strictly follow the rules and regulations on the disbursement of funds with complete documentation to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommend that the LCE direct the LDRRFO and Municipal Accountant to submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Fund Project (LDRRMFP) and the monthly report of Source and Utilization of DRRMF and strictly observe the disclosure requirements of Unexpended balance of Quick Response Fund (QRF) and the MOOE of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statement.
We recommend that Head of Agency shall strictly enforce the compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of RRSA, Section 43 and 44 of LGU NGAS Vol. I.
